name: Cinnamon's Restaurant
tagline: Kailua brunch institution where the guava chiffon pancakes and red velvet pancakes have a cult following.
mustOrder: Guava chiffon pancakes — fluffy, tangy-sweet, the signature dish
Red velvet pancakes — cream cheese frosting on pancakes, outrageously good
Eggs Benedict — local twist with kalua pork or crab cake
parking: Parking lot behind the restaurant off Kailua Rd. Can fill up on weekends — arrive before 8:30am or after 11am.
talkStory: Opened in 1985 as a tiny breakfast counter, Cinnamon's later moved to a bigger Kailua Road location to handle the crowds — but weekends still pack out. The guava chiffon pancakes, made with real guava puree folded into the batter, are the signature that built a following across three decades. Named a Hale Aina Award winner multiple times. The move: brunch at Cinnamon's, then walk to Kailua Beach. Grab a patio table if you can — it gets warm inside.
